Rheumatoid arthritis (RA) autoimmune disease which affect people age.
causes hot, painful, stiff swollen joints, typically starting small joints hands feet.
normal role your body's immune system fight infections keep healthy.
autoimmune disease, your immune system starts attacking your healthy tissues.
immune system targets lining joints, causing inflammation joint damage.
usually affects smaller joints, such joints hands feet.
However larger joints such hips knees also affected.
Rheumatoid arthritis affects nearly half million Australians.
present, causes rheumatoid arthritis fully understood research continues.
Rheumatoid arthritis also triggered other factors, including smoking range infections viruses.
Whatever starts process, main problem rheumatoid arthritis that your body's immune system begins target your joints.
While immune system usually protects against infections, rheumatoid arthritis mistakenly treats tissue your joints 'foreign'.
resulting inflammation lead painful symptoms arthritis permanent damage your bones joints untreated.
Damage your joints reduced with early ongoing treatment.
Joint pain, swelling, tenderness touch.
Stiffness joints, especially morning.
Joints being affected symmetrically (i.e.
same joints both sides body).
will doctor diagnose rheumatoid arthritis?
There single test rheumatoid arthritis.
Because early diagnosis correct treatment reduce impact disease, most people with symptoms rheumatoid arthritis should visit specialist doctor rheumatologist) soon possible.
Specialists look results from range tests help them decide whether person rheumatoid arthritis.
Talk about your symptoms, including where when feel joint pain.
Check whether have fevers, lost weight been very tired.
Examine your hands, feet other joints swelling, heat, tenderness bumps, called 'nodules'.
Take blood sample check whether your body's immune system activated whether attacking your joints.
Possibly needle take small amount fluid from your affected joints.
Send radiologist x-ray other scan, such ultrasound, affected joints.
more information download booklet from Arthritis Australia, which serves practical guide treatments, services lifestyle choices.
Living with rheumatoid arthritis challenging times does affect different people different ways.
some cases, disease disappear, come ('flare') many years.
other people, symptoms disability slowly worsen over time.
left untreated, rheumatoid arthritis lead damage joints that cannot repaired.
Other parts body also affected, such your lungs your eyes.
Fortunately, these problems outside your joints uncommon risk reduced dramatically with treatment.
Joint inflammation initially cause ongoing pain some degree disability, which affect your ability work undertake home duties.
disease cannot 'cured' present, most people controlled with ongoing care.
Many people achieve remission, which goal treatment control symptoms return normal function.
further information living well with rheumatoid arthritis, click here view Rheumatoid Arthritis booklet.
Will medicine cure rheumatoid arthritis?
present, there 'cure' rheumatoid arthritis.
However, early right medicines, discussed speakers, slow down damage caused disease, relieve pain stiffness reduce long-term disability.
treatment achieve remission return your normal activities many people this achievable.
Many arthritis medicines need taken regularly work properly should stopped suddenly talk your doctor have concerns about side effects, safety cost.
have heard speaker interviews, each person responds differently arthritis medicines, which means that will need work with your specialist find best medications doses you.
This take time, finding most effective medicines with least side effects, hope really make difference controlling your rheumatoid arthritis.
Drugs such these helpful arthritis joints your arms legs, although they're usually effective spinal symptoms.
When taking DMARDs, you'll need regular check-ups blood tests monitor their effect.
Methotrexate most commonly prescribed DMARD's.
This safe effective drug been used first line therapy (and other forms inflammatory arthritis) decades.
Originally developed chemotherapy methotrexate used very small doses works 'dampen down' modulate overactive immune system, thereby reducing systemic inflammation.
Taken own, conjunction with other DMARD's, methotrexate been shown slow down progression disease thus preventing minimising damage joints, well reducing symptoms such swelling, pain, morning stiffness fatigue.
dose methotrexate very effective treatment many people with taken directed, enhance mobility function improve quality life.
Biological therapies relatively treatments that very effective rheumatoid arthritis.
number drugs currently available rheumatoid arthritis.
These biological therapies currently only available patients with more severe forms condition can't controlled with anti-inflammatory drugs they suitable everyone.
effect these drugs will monitored, you'll need complete questionnaires regularly which assess active your disease well it's responding treatment.
Pacing alternating heavy repeated tasks with lighter tasks.
Taking break reduces stress painful joints conserves energy allowing weakened muscles rest.
Positioning joints wisely promotes proper alignment decrease excess stress.
example, squatting kneeling extra stress your hips knees.
When lifting carrying heavy items, keep items waist height avoid carrying them down stairs.
Talk your doctor about seeing occupational therapist physiotherapist, prescribe splints, braces orthotics (shoe inserts) help align support your joints.
Using assistive devices conserves energy makes daily tasks easier.
Raise seat levels decrease stress knee joints.
reacher pick items from ground.
cane decrease stress knee joints.
Enlarge grips utensils, such spoons peelers, decrease stress delicate hand joints.
Other devices consider include carts carrying objects jar/tap openers.
additional information living home with arthritis, review Arthritis Australia's home with arthritis' which outlines range simple steps managing home.
planning baby, discuss your plans with your doctor beforehand some drugs temporarily reduce fertility.
Other drugs, such methotrexate leflunomide, affect baby's development it's important reliable contraception while you're taking these this applies wanting father child well women.
Most mothers with rheumatoid arthritis feel better during pregnancy, possibly because activity immune system reduced during pregnancy 'tolerate' growing baby.
Symptoms likely return once baby born.
However, flare-up symptoms after birth usually dealt with quickly.
'Arthritis, Pregnancy Path Parenthood', published Suzie May, details Suzie's experience young Australian woman with rheumatoid arthritis, along with experiences other women from around world they fulfil their goal becoming parents, despite their arthritis.
This practical guide help understand potential challenges face give strategies overcome these challenges.
book available here.
addition, women, Arthritis Australia collated personal stories range women living with arthritis, known 'Women's Insights into Rheumatoid Arthritis'.
There many resources available help people with rheumatoid arthritis.
Your doctor touch with social worker, help explain financial health services that available you.
These include pensions allowances that might entitled plus financial assistance such Health Care Concession Cards low-cost treatment programs.
Your local council, community health centre, community group religious organisation also offer programs that include practical advice, activities, social networks just someone talk
Arthritis Australia always here help too.
Give them chance talk they might have worries feel that they don't know enough about your disease affecting you.
Visit your worried about well coping, your able suggest additional ways coping, prescribe medicines especially worried depressed.
Many people with rheumatoid arthritis will also visit physiotherapist (physio).
These practitioners various treatments, including exercise therapy hydrotherapy (water exercise), keep your joints flexible, strong pain-free possible.
They will also show exercises pain-relief techniques home.
might also visit occupational therapist (OT), they come your home work.
provide advice things that reduces joint strain pain teach strategies protect mobility your joints, particularly those your hands.
They also suggest changes your house such taps aids such splints that make life easier protect your joints.
podiatrist help take care your feet.
They find ways reduce pain your toes, knees hips, perhaps providing shoe inserts advice footwear.
Thank Arthritis Society (Canada) Arthritis Research contribution content materials above overview.

Erdogan's comments come shortly after accusations that Turkey been supporting radical factions Syrian opposition al-Qaeda affiliates Syria, specifically Al-Nusra Islamic State Iraq Sham.
groups have gained prominence throughout course Syrian conflict, emerging part resistance against Syrian President Bashir al-Assad.
Over past several months, groups have gained ground northern part Syria along Turkish-Syrian border.
beginning Syrian conflict, Erdogan's government strongest supporters Syrian opposition repeatedly called Al-Assad step down.
Turkish government openly declared support Free Syrian Army, which affiliated with Istanbul-based Syrian National Coalition.
Ankara also kept open-door policy throughout more than year-long Syrian conflict, providing lifeline rebel-controlled areas.
However, past years Turkish government repeatedly denied supporting Syrian groups that radical nature.
Still, news reports have claimed that Turkey provides support radical Islamist groups.
August interview with Turkish publication Taraf, Saleh Muslim, leader Kurdish Syrian group known Democratic Union Party, said that group intelligence confirming transfers ammunition between Turkish Syrian borders.
Muslim added that al-Nusra group picked weapons preparation attack Kurdish villages just over border northern Syria.
While Muslim's comments directly implicate Turkish government supporting radical groups, frustration seemed stem from Erdogan government's turning blind towards al-Qaeda affiliated groups allowing them conduct operations Turkish soil largely uninhibited.
Recently, Turkey seems have changed diplomatic tone towards Syria.
This change comes following series incidents that occurred over last months around Turkish-Syrian border, namely bombing Ryhanli, Turkey this past May.
attack, allegedly carried those linked Syrian intelligence agency, left more than people dead drew verbal outcry from Turkish public opposition parties, blamed bombing partially Turkish government weak diplomacy with Syria.
Turkish people also expressed frustration what they passive attitude from United States Turkish-Syrian conflict.
recent deal between United States Russia that will dismantle Syria's chemical weapons instead pursuing military action against Al-Assad left Turkey isolated efforts forcefully intervene Syrian politics oust Al-Assad.
positive development, Hassan Rouhani's July election Iranian presidency presented Ankara with rare opportunity collaborate with moderate political figure unlike predecessor.
two-pronged approach Turkey Iran engaging Syria could prove effective quelling Syria's violence.
What many consider Turkey's relaxed policy toward al-Qaeda affiliates have targeted Kurdish villages Syrian Kurdish groups been threatening peaceful talks between Ankara outlawed Kurdish Worker's Party.
These talks were designed decades-long Kurdish conflict that began 1984 since resulted death more than 40,000 people.
endangering these talks, Turkish government compromising 10-month ceasefire with Kurdistan Workers Party, period during which single Turkish soldier died.
ceasefire talks have been highly welcomed development most Turkish public.
Losing these negotiations could, according both European Union United States, threaten Justice Development Party's chances staying power following country's next major elections.
likely that with recent bombing outcry against Turkish government, Erdogan's team been reevaluating approach handling Syrian conflict.
October Turkish army reported that fired strongholds Islamic State Iraq Syria retaliation insurgent group's mortar attacks from hideaways Syria's mountain ranges.
This marked first retaliations Turkish forces targeting al-Qaeda affiliate.
October Turkish government announced that frozen assets businessmen with links al-Qaeda Taliban groups.
November Turkish police seized cargo truck full rocket heads, bazookas, missiles, bombs guns near southern province Adana.
truck believed have been route al-Qaeda affiliates based Syria.
Some argue that revival Turkish policies designed quell radical groups' activities could clear Turkey from allegations that assisting them.
Analysts warn, however, that this could also lead Turkey becoming target radical groups.
"Turkey's going public with identity target could introduce element recently heightened accusations Turkey helping radical groups," said writer Nihat Ozcan October article website Al-Monitor.
this "could also make Turkey target, especially al-Qaeda affiliates," added.
Just weeks after Ozcan's statements, Today's Zaman newspaper published report suggesting that al-Qaeda-linked Islamic State Iraq Syria planning target major Turkish cities, citing reports from Turkish intelligence officials.
"Their vehicles being prepared Syria's northern city Raqqa," stated report.
"Intelligence units discovered detailed information Oct. about ISIS's plan carry terrorist attack, contact with local units."
Following Today's Zaman report, Islamic State Iraq Syria allegedly issued statement from Mohammed Al-Adnani, spokesperson group.
statement, denied that group plans attack Turkey other countries.
"There truth supposed plans target Turkish cities," said Aymen Jawad Al-Tamimi, fellow Middle East Forum, emailed comment Atlantic Post.
"The alleged statements fabrications from pro-Assad circles," said, "who aiming cajole Turkey into tightening security border stop inflow foreign fighters into Syria."
Al-Tamimi argued that Turkey "does anything" stop flow foreign fighters into Syria join ISIS al-Nusra.
said Turkey "sees these groups useful proxies against [Democratic Union Party] they have actually threatened attack Turkey."
However, according recent comments Democratic Union Party leaders, Ankara believed have stopped flow al-Qaeda fighters crossing into Syria.
"The [radical groups] attacking after crossing [Syria] from Turkey before," said Saleh Muslim November, according Turkish publication Taraf.
"This very good news.
hope that Turkey will continue this stance [towards extremist groups]."
Aaron Stein, associate fellow Royal United Services Institute Defense Security Studies Ph.D candidate King's University told Atlantic Post interview this month that still early tell, though Saleh Muslim's recent assertion that foreign fighters have entered from Turkey fight [Democratic Union Party] noteworthy."
Turkey's border areas, said, still flooded with Syrian foreign fighters, adding that would unrealistic assume that Ankara simply make them away overnight."
Whether Turkey supported radical Syrian rebels least provided lifeline them past, many believe, Stein agrees that Ankara definitely rethinking foreign policy strategy result complications with radical groups region.
wouldn't that Turkey adopted Syria policy rather taking pragmatic steps prepare future filled with conflict," said Stein.
"Ankara's recent moves Iraq Iran aren't really foreign policy reset, rather pragmatic smart step compartmentalize Turkey's relationship with states region."
